description:
You are acting as a junior compliance-operations analyst who has just received three
    raw authentication log files that cover the period 2023-05-01 → 2023-05-03.
    Your team lead needs an audit-ready evidence set that shows  
    1) every failed login during that window,  
    2) a per-day count of those failures, and  
    3) the list of unique users who experienced failures.

    The raw input files already exist on disk:

      • /home/user/data/logs/auth_2023-05-01.log  
      • /home/user/data/logs/auth_2023-05-02.log  
      • /home/user/data/logs/auth_2023-05-03.log  

    Each line in the raw files has this exact key-value layout
        YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ user=<username> action=login status=<status> ip=<ipv4>

    Your tasks (execute them in the order given):

    1) Create a new directory that will hold all artefacts:
          /home/user/audit
       The directory must exist when you are finished.

    2) Extract every line whose “status=” field equals the literal value “failed”
       from all three raw files (inclusive date range 2023-05-01 → 2023-05-03).
       Preserve the original chronological order of those lines and concatenate
       them into one file named

          /home/user/audit/failed_2023-05-01_2023-05-03.log

       Output rules for this file:
         • Plain UTF-8 text.  
         • One full original log line per line.  
         • No extra characters or columns.  
         • The file must end with a single trailing newline (Unix “\n”).  

    3) Produce a daily summary CSV called

          /home/user/audit/failed_summary.csv

       Format requirements:
         • UTF-8, comma-separated, **no** spaces around commas.  
         • Column-header row must be literally:
               date,total_failed
         • One subsequent row per calendar date present in the extraction
           (ascending date order).  
         • Dates must be in ISO form YYYY-MM-DD.  
         • The file must also end with a single trailing newline.  

    4) Produce an alphabetical list of unique users that appear in the failed
       lines.  Save the list to

          /home/user/audit/unique_failed_users.txt

       Formatting rules:
         • Strict ASCII / UTF-8.  
         • One username per line.  
         • Pure lexicographical (ASCIIbetical) order, “a” → “z”.  
         • No header line and no blank lines.  
         • File must end with exactly one trailing newline.

    5) When everything is finished, print the absolute paths of the three
       generated artefacts (one path per line, in any order) to STDOUT so that
       the automated checker can easily read them.

    Notes & constraints:
      • You do NOT need root privileges; all work must be done as the normal user.
      • Do not hard-code values—derive them by processing the input data.
      • Be sure every required file exists and matches the stated format
        specifications, otherwise the automated tests will fail.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth fixture data and the expected artefacts.
        These values are hidden from the agent and used solely by the test-suite.
    -->

    <!-- 1. Initial input files and their exact contents -->
    <inputs>
        <file path="/home/user/data/logs/auth_2023-05-01.log"><![CDATA[
2023-05-01T00:11:04Z user=alice action=login status=success ip=10.0.0.5
2023-05-01T01:45:33Z user=bob action=login status=failed ip=10.0.0.7
2023-05-01T02:17:54Z user=charlie action=login status=failed ip=10.0.0.9
2023-05-01T03:19:54Z user=alice action=logout status=success ip=10.0.0.5
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/data/logs/auth_2023-05-02.log"><![CDATA[
2023-05-02T04:22:10Z user=jdoe action=login status=failed ip=10.0.0.13
2023-05-02T05:24:10Z user=bob action=login status=success ip=10.0.0.7
2023-05-02T05:55:01Z user=erin action=login status=failed ip=10.0.0.11
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/data/logs/auth_2023-05-03.log"><![CDATA[
2023-05-03T06:30:00Z user=frank action=login status=failed ip=10.0.0.15
2023-05-03T07:31:00Z user=charlie action=login status=failed ip=10.0.0.9
2023-05-03T08:32:00Z user=jdoe action=login status=failed ip=10.0.0.13
2023-05-03T09:33:00Z user=george action=login status=success ip=10.0.0.20
]]></file>
    </inputs>

    <!-- 2. Expected files after the agent completes the task -->
    <expected>
        <file path="/home/user/audit/failed_2023-05-01_2023-05-03.log"><![CDATA[
2023-05-01T01:45:33Z user=bob action=login status=failed ip=10.0.0.7
2023-05-01T02:17:54Z user=charlie action=login status=failed ip=10.0.0.9
2023-05-02T04:22:10Z user=jdoe action=login status=failed ip=10.0.0.13
2023-05-02T05:55:01Z user=erin action=login status=failed ip=10.0.0.11
2023-05-03T06:30:00Z user=frank action=login status=failed ip=10.0.0.15
2023-05-03T07:31:00Z user=charlie action=login status=failed ip=10.0.0.9
2023-05-03T08:32:00Z user=jdoe action=login status=failed ip=10.0.0.13
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/audit/failed_summary.csv"><![CDATA[
date,total_failed
2023-05-01,2
2023-05-02,2
2023-05-03,3
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/audit/unique_failed_users.txt"><![CDATA[
bob
charlie
erin
frank
jdoe
]]></file>
    </expected>
